details: Created core directory structure: src/, bots/, memory/, task-ledger/, .agents/, .gemini/, tests/unit, tests/integration, tests/functional
Extended .env.example with appointment system variables: Google OAuth2, SendGrid, Odoo, CLI orchestration, memory system
Created memory system: README.md, current/ files (now.md, next-actions.md, blockers.md, active-contracts.md, active-risks.md), architecture/system-map.md, providers/gemini/gemini-ops.md
Extended AGENTS.md with appointment-system-rules section including core architecture, API contract, security rules, Odoo integration rules, memory system rules, CLI orchestration rules
Updated CLAUDE.md with Mega Prompt context, created GEMINI.md with modular imports
Created task-ledger base files: tasks.json with Phase 0 tasks, tasks.md human-readable, execution-log.ndjson, handoffs.json
Phase 0 Setup completed successfully. All directory structures, configuration files, memory system, and task ledger initialized. Ready for Phase 1A - Google Calendar Core
Created src/config/googleAuth.js with OAuth2 client configuration, token lifecycle management, automatic refresh, and error handling for invalid_grant
Created src/services/calendarService.js with createCalendarEvent function for Google Calendar API integration, including event creation, error handling, and future methods for get/update/delete
Created src/services/emailService.js with SendGrid integration for appointment confirmations, including HTML/text email templates, calendar links, and error handling for API codes
Created src/utils/validation.js with appointment payload validation, email/date/time format checks, sanitization functions, and Zod-like validation structure
Created src/utils/dateTime.js with ISO 8601 conversion utilities, end time calculation, timezone handling, and date validation functions
Created src/utils/logger.js with structured logging, service operation tracking, error logging with context, and configurable log levels
Created app.js Express server with middleware stack, appointment routes, health check endpoint, and error handling. Created src/routes/appointmentRoutes.js with async error wrappers

Created bots/setupAndTestBot.js with comprehensive environment verification (Node.js, dependencies, .env, OAuth tokens, SendGrid, directories, memory system) and functional tests. Installed required dependencies, configured .env, executed bot successfully - all 15 checks/tests passed
Created validation.js (payload validation/sanitization), dateTime.js (ISO UTC conversion, business hours), logger.js (structured logging with levels and file output) in src/utils/
Created errorHandler.js (global error handling with specific error types) and notFound.js (404 middleware) in src/middlewares/
Created src/controllers/appointmentController.js with createAppointment function orchestrating validation, calendar creation, email sending, and error handling

Phase 1A - Google Calendar Core completed successfully. All 6 tasks (GC-001 to GC-006) implemented with OAuth2, calendar service, email service, validation/utils, middlewares, and controller. Ready for validation and testing before Phase 1B (Odoo Integration)
Created src/routes/appointmentRoutes.js with Express routes for appointments API and app.js with full Express server setup including middlewares, health check, and error handling
Phase 1A - Google Calendar Core: 5/6 tasks completed (GC-001 to GC-005). Remaining: GC-006 (Controller)
Phase 1A - Google Calendar Core: 4/6 tasks completed (GC-001 to GC-004). Remaining: GC-005 (Middlewares), GC-006 (Controller)
Started Odoo preparation by validating the repo state, confirming local Odoo 19 compatibility with JSON-2, and designing the shared appointment-side Odoo client configuration.

Created src/config/odooClient.js with JSON-2 config validation, bearer auth headers, endpoint builders, timeout-aware request helpers, and unit coverage. Updated memory/current files and the task ledger for the Odoo preparation phase.
Integrated Odoo sync into the shared appointment flow, added lib/odoo-sync.ts, returned odooAppointmentId in appointment responses, and refactored /api/contact to use the shared flow.
Extended the Odoo addon with model galante.appointment, access rules, and the create_from_api action to perform idempotent partner lookup/create plus appointment persistence.

Validated the integrated appointment flow with unit tests, Next.js production build, node-based functional tests including odoo_error degradation, and Selenium E2E using the local Chrome profile pattern.

Reworked the public contact form to load available slots from the backend, added admin-configurable booking window fields (start, end, interval, weekdays), improved user-facing booking errors, and revalidated Selenium E2E with the slot picker UI.

Repaired production Odoo host routing by updating infra/nginx/nginx.conf to define connection_upgrade, include /etc/nginx/conf.d/*.conf, and avoid a conflicting base default_server. Recreated only galantes_nginx on the GCP VM, verified nginx healthy, confirmed public /web/login returns Odoo 200 OK, and passed Selenium smoke tests/e2e/odoo_public_login_smoke.py.

Completed production web hardening and validation: blocked product image path traversal with numeric ID validation and cache path confinement, added auth route rate limits, enabled security headers and restricted image remote patterns, validated lint, TypeScript, build, and 93 Vitest tests, selectively rebuilt galantes_web on GCP, verified public health/security headers/Odoo routing, and passed Selenium Profile 9 product catalog plus account navbar smokes.

Closed additional pending hygiene tasks: confirmed admin Odoo sync observability and filtering in the appointments panel/API, added backend route tests for valid/invalid odooSyncStatus filters and unauthorized access, verified predeploy backup rotation via KEEP_BACKUPS in a temporary directory, added .gitattributes LF normalization, and validated focused tests plus TypeScript.

Improved checkout fulfillment for signed-in customers: added account profile GET preload, checkout autocompletion, saved address cards, top-level Ship insured vs Boutique pickup choice, server-side pickup support without shipping address, focused unit and Playwright coverage, production web-only deploy with backup, health validation, and production Playwright UI verification with intercepted APIs.

Closed checkout edge cases after deployment: Use a new address now clears previously selected address fields, Stripe checkout rejects pickup rates unless deliveryMethod is explicitly pickup, GET /api/account/profile has focused unit coverage, and the update was validated locally then deployed web-only with backup and rechecked on production via health and Playwright API-intercepted UI verification.

Implemented the GetUpSoft + EasyCount transformation plan as an executable ledger phase GUS_EASYCNT with 31 tasks, priorities, effort estimates, dependencies, assignees, and task-level skill mappings. Created docs/getupsoft-easycount-transformation-roadmap.md and updated task-ledger/tasks.md with the initial ready queue.

Created and scaffolded the four private GitHub repositories under JoelStalin, generated ADRs, OpenAPI v1, RACI, 90-day roadmap, Go/No-Go checklist, risk register, rollback runbook, variable matrix, brand guidelines, site architecture, resilience design, benchmark, final blueprint, Cloudflare provisioning script, and reports. GUS-006 and GUS-017 remain blocked pending a valid Cloudflare token and final secret values.

Validated task-ledger/tasks.json and task-ledger/execution-log.ndjson parsing, confirmed all four GitHub repos are private and have README/CODEOWNERS/PR template/issue template/CI workflow scaffold, confirmed repository URL variables are present, validated PowerShell syntax for scripts/provision-getupsoft-cloudflare.ps1, ran TypeScript successfully, and ran lint with two pre-existing warnings. Full Vitest run produced three timeout failures in existing tests unrelated to the new documentation/provisioning artifacts.

Added a Scrapling StealthyFetcher automation helper at scripts/scrapling_stealth_fetch.py, documented usage in docs/automation/scrapling-stealth.md, added agent guidance in AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md, added scrapling[fetchers] to tests/e2e/requirements.txt, and validated with py_compile, unittest, and CLI help output.

Ran functional validation for this conversation's deliverables: ledger JSON/NDJSON parse passed; all expected ADR/GetUpSoft/EasyCount/Scrapling docs exist; OpenAPI smoke passed; Cloudflare provisioner syntax and DryRun passed; all four GitHub repos are private and have README, CODEOWNERS, PR template, issue template, CI workflow, and URL variables; Scrapling unit tests passed; a real Scrapling fetch against https://example.com extracted 'Example Domain' after installing Scrapling and Playwright Chromium. Global Python cleanup restored pip check to no broken requirements. Cloudflare live zone lookup still returns HTTP 400 with the local token, so live DNS remains blocked.

Corrected GetUpSoft/EasyCount deployment target to ssh.getupsoft.com.do with isolation rules for a shared server. Added docs/getupsoft-easycount/deployment-isolation.md, updated blueprint/Cloudflare/variable docs, changed Cloudflare provisioning to target ssh.getupsoft.com.do, added scripts/check-getupsoft-deploy-target.ps1, set non-secret deployment variables across the four GitHub repos, and ran a non-mutating SSH preflight. The server has many active Docker projects, including galantes-staging and /opt/EasyCounting project server; selected GetUpSoft ports 3120, 3110, 3200, 3210, 3300, and 3310 are free, and /srv/getupsoft target paths are currently missing.

Updated GetUpSoft runtime notes after the user reported the corporate portal is already functioning. From this workspace, https://getupsoft.com.do returned HTTP 200 with the title 'Certia | Plataforma Corporativa'. Apex getupsoft.com still could not be resolved from this environment, so the docs now mark the corporate portal as live while keeping apex DNS verification pending.

Created and scaffolded the private repository JoelStalin/getupsoft-admin-portals with README, CODEOWNERS, issue template, PR template, CI workflow, and non-secret deployment variables for ssh.getupsoft.com.do. Updated ADRs and deployment-isolation docs so admin.getupsoft.com and admin.getupsoft.com.do are explicit router targets on isolated port 3400.

Reviewed the shared host design for ssh.getupsoft.com.do. The preflight still shows no /srv/getupsoft/* deployment paths exist, while many unrelated Docker projects are active on the server. Public verification shows getupsoft.com.do serves a corporate page through Cloudflare, but apex getupsoft.com still lacks public DNS answers from this environment. Current evidence indicates the live corporate page is being served by an existing stack, not by a newly isolated GetUpSoft deployment under /srv/getupsoft.

Recovered stable SSH access to ssh.getupsoft.com.do through an explicit cloudflared access TCP tunnel on 127.0.0.1:9222, inspected the live server project at /opt/EasyCounting/deploy/server, and confirmed the active router lives in nginx.conf mounted into server-nginx-1. Backed up nginx.conf, added global aliases for getupsoft.com, www.getupsoft.com, admin.getupsoft.com, and easycount.getupsoft.com alongside the existing .com.do routes, validated nginx syntax, and reloaded only server-nginx-1. Functional host-header checks against http://127.0.0.1:80 returned HTTP 200 with the expected Certia titles for corporate, admin, and client portals.

Added automated GetUpSoft portal validation using Selenium, Scrapling, and n8n orchestration. Created tests/e2e/getupsoft_portals_suite.py with the cloned-profile Selenium runtime, scripts/getupsoft_portals_scrapling_smoke.py, tests/e2e/getupsoft_portal_targets.py, automation/n8n/getupsoft-portals-regression-workflow.json, scripts/setup_scrapling_env.ps1, and docs/automation/getupsoft-portals-regression.md. Validated unit tests, restored the global Python environment to a clean pip check state, and confirmed the new suites pass for getupsoft.com.do and admin.getupsoft.com.do while correctly reporting easycount.getupsoft.com.do as blocked pending public DNS visibility.
